trivia_bullets.text: Core Set 2021 (released July 3, 2020) centers on Teferi and reintroduced phasing to Standard for the first time since the Weatherlight era. Roaming Ghostlight is a blue common Spirit — the Spirit creature type has recurring thematic importance in blue across MTG sets, generally representing restless or undirected souls [Scryfall].

Roaming Ghostlight is a first printing — the card has appeared in no other set in the Scryfall prints database. Notably, it was reprinted in Ravnica: Clue Edition (CLU, 2024), making it one of the handful of M21 commons that found a second life in a mystery-themed crossover product [Scryfall prints_search_uri; frontmatter note].

Artist Zezhou Chen illustrated Roaming Ghostlight. According to Hipsters of the Coast, Wardscale Crocodile (War of the Spark) was Chen's first-ever traditional painting for Magic — prior to that card he had illustrated 37 cards digitally. Roaming Ghostlight is among his digital works [Hipsters of the Coast, April 2019 article].
sources: Hipsters of the Coast — 'Art for Wardscale Crocodile by Zezhou Chen Sells for $1,500' (2019)

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Some who die senseless deaths return as formless spirits, seeking answers where there are none." — no attributed speaker; the line frames the spirit as driven by unresolvable grief rather than malice [Scryfall].

EDHREC rank 26,349. The bounce-on-enter trigger (return a non-Spirit to hand) reads as a rescue effect for Spirit tribal and a tempo disruptor in Pauper blue builds. At {3}{U}{U} it is expensive for a 3/2 common, which limits its Commander appeal; in Pauper blue-skies or Spirit-tribal shells it can provide targeted bounce while contributing a flying body [Scryfall EDHREC rank field; Scryfall legalities].
